ACM CSRP

Fifth Biannual Series

Tell Me More

Events

We host research-focused sessions where faculty and students share published work, discuss influential papers, and occasionally run hands-on workshops. Come to learn what’s new, ask good questions, and leave with ideas you can use in your own projects.

Faculty Research Talks

Faculty present their published and in-progress research with a focus on the core idea, key results, and takeaways. Q&A includes methods, limitations, and open problems—plus how students can connect or collaborate.

Student Research Talks

Students share accepted papers, posters, or research milestones, and sometimes a ‘what I learned’ talk after reading a landmark paper. Great for practice, feedback, and building research confidence.

Workshops

Occasional skill sessions on tools and habits that make research smoother—literature search, LaTeX, reproducibility, peer review, research storytelling, and presenting technical work clearly.

Research Ambassadors

Step into the spotlight once, join us for 8 more talks and present once, and graduate the semester as a Research Ambassador, with a certificate to celebrate your research engagement.

About Us

ACM CSRP presents the Research Presentation Series, your weekly window into what’s happening across Computer Science.

Each week, a student or faculty member highlights a recently published paper or a high-impact research result, followed by an open discussion on the key ideas, evidence, limitations, and future directions. After every session, we stick around to socialize, connect with presenters, and meet other research-minded students.

The series is a Qualified Honors College “think” experience!

CSRP is a great way to build research confidence and community: sharpen how you read and discuss papers, learn about research opportunities in the CS Department (including paid roles), add a strong line to your CV/resume, generate new project ideas, and spark new collaborations—all in a welcoming, low-pressure environment.

Spring 2026 Schedule

Weekly talks and discussions (Fridays). Topics marked TBA will be updated as speakers finalize details.

  • Jan 30, 2026

    Introduction & News

    Presenter: Dr. Nick Ivanov

  • Feb 6, 2026

    Smart Spaces, Private Lives: A Culturally Grounded Examination of Privacy Tensions in Smart Homes

    Presenter: Shai Zayden

  • Feb 13, 2026

    Confidential Computing within an AI Accelerator

    Presenter: Timmy Nguyen

  • Feb 20, 2026

    Measuring NIST Authentication Standards Compliance by Higher Education Institutions

    Presenter: Doug Tranz

  • Feb 27, 2026

    TBA

    Presenter: Sushanth Ambati

  • Mar 6, 2026

    Effective Immersive Analytics for Everyday Use

    Presenter: Prof. Benjamin Weidner

  • Mar 13, 2026

    TBA

    Presenter: Drew Dillman

  • Mar 20, 2026

    No Presentation — Spring Break

    Enjoy the break! We’ll resume the following week.

  • Mar 27, 2026

    TBA

    Presenter: Jonah Rodriguez

  • Apr 3, 2026

    Change Point Detection in Evolving Graph using Martingale

    Presenter: Tarun Teja

  • Apr 10, 2026

    Bound-Tightened Densest Subgraph Discovery on GPU

    Presenter: Wajid Manzoor

  • Apr 17, 2026

    TBA

    Presenter: James Fogg

  • Apr 24, 2026

    TBA

    Presenter: Aneesh Anand

  • See you!

Attend Our Talks

Join us Fridays at 3:00 PM in the CS Department’s Robinson Hall (302) for our weekly talks and discussions.

Weekly schedule and room details for in-person attendance.

When
Fridays at 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Spring 2026)
Where

MEET THE TEAM

A group of students and faculty building a stronger research community.

Faculty Advisor

Wajid Manzoor

Vinay Kumar Uppalapati

Student Organizer

We organize weekly talks, share opportunities, and connect students with labs, mentors, and collaborators across Rowan. Want to present, suggest a topic, or be part of the team? Reach out, Contact us we’re always looking for more help.

Contact Us

Want to present a paper, suggest a speaker, or get involved? Email the right person below.
